schema.name: TZPID source truth registry gap fill
schema.basis: folder-local candidate promotion with safety gate

theory.canonical_statement: The second Untitled6 row is repaired as the same source-backed qubit superposition relation with explicit amplitude normalization.
theory.technical_interpretation: The equation support layer follows \lvert0\rangle,\lvert1\rangle\in\mathcal{H}_2; \lvert\psi\rangle=\alpha\lvert0\rangle+\beta\lvert1\rangle; \lvert\alpha\rvert^2+\lvert\beta\rvert^2=1. Each relation is retained as a concise mathematical object for folder-local production preview.

dictionary_sections.definition: Qubit Superposition State II is a source-backed registry entry anchored by \lvert\psi\rangle=\alpha\lvert0\rangle+\beta\lvert1\rangle.
dictionary_sections.contextual_meaning: The second Untitled6 row is repaired as the same source-backed qubit superposition relation with explicit amplitude normalization. The packet keeps the entry usable in the four-page production layout without inventing unsupported material.
dictionary_sections.derivable_consequences: The canonical relation yields a symbol key, an equation-support chain, and a compact Wolfram model suitable for review before source-truth promotion.
dictionary_sections.lemma_support: Source row extraction; canonical-equation normalization; symbol-key grounding; TRAWIN closure compatibility.
dictionary_sections.technical_interpretation: The equation support layer follows \lvert0\rangle,\lvert1\rangle\in\mathcal{H}_2; \lvert\psi\rangle=\alpha\lvert0\rangle+\beta\lvert1\rangle; \lvert\alpha\rvert^2+\lvert\beta\rvert^2=1. Each relation is retained as a concise mathematical object for folder-local production preview.
dictionary_sections.equation_grounding: Grounded source: `D:\Conversation\2-Ready-For-Extraction\pdf\TZPID\id_field_assignments.csv`. The canonical equation is \lvert\psi\rangle=\alpha\lvert0\rangle+\beta\lvert1\rangle.
dictionary_sections.interpretive_role: This repair converts a malformed or empty registry row into a source-aligned production packet while preserving the approval boundary.
dictionary_sections.tzp_thesaurus:
\begin{enumerate}[leftmargin=1.6em,itemsep=6pt,topsep=4pt]
\item Canonical carrier: the repaired mathematical relation used as the spine of the entry.
\item Source row: the D-drive assignment row that supplies the equation or edge.
\item TRAWIN closure: the interpretation through time, rotation, action, wave, integration, and normalization.
\end{enumerate}
dictionary_sections.encyclopedia_mathematica: A suitable visual should show the canonical relation at center, the symbol key as labeled inputs, and the equation-support chain as the derivation path.

publication_overrides.equation_support_summary: The canonical relation yields a symbol key, an equation-support chain, and a compact Wolfram model suitable for review before source-truth promotion.

derivation_layer.source_latex:
% BEGIN TZP DERIVATION LAYER
\tzpsection{Derivation Layer}
\begin{enumerate}[leftmargin=1.6em,itemsep=7pt,topsep=4pt]
\item
\[
\lvert0\rangle,\lvert1\rangle\in\mathcal{H}_2
\]
This relation is retained from the source-backed repair chain.
\item
\[
\lvert\psi\rangle=\alpha\lvert0\rangle+\beta\lvert1\rangle
\]
This relation is retained from the source-backed repair chain.
\item
\[
\lvert\alpha\rvert^2+\lvert\beta\rvert^2=1
\]
This relation is retained from the source-backed repair chain.
\end{enumerate}
% END TZP DERIVATION LAYER
